items.title: ClaudeHook
items.text: Do interesting stuff in Claude Code hooks

items.title: 🔥 Usage
items.text:
Program : ClaudeHook  by peter@forret.com
Version : v0.0.1 (Apr 22 16:07:13 2023)
Purpose : Do interesting stuff in Claude Code hooks
Usage   : ClaudeHook [-h] [-q] [-v] [-f] [-l <log_dir>] [-t <tmp_dir>] <action>
Flags, options and parameters:
    -h|--help        : [flag] show usage [default: off]
    -q|--quiet       : [flag] no output [default: off]
    -v|--verbose     : [flag] also show debug messages [default: off]
    -f|--force       : [flag] do not ask for confirmation (always yes) [default: off]
    -l|--log_dir <?> : [option] folder for log files   [default: /Users/pforret/log/script]
    -t|--tmp_dir <?> : [option] folder for temp files  [default: /tmp/script]
    <action>         : [choice] action to perform  [options: action1,action2,check,env,update]

### TIPS & EXAMPLES
* use ClaudeHook action1 to ...
  ClaudeHook action1
* use ClaudeHook action2 to ...
  ClaudeHook action2
* use ClaudeHook check to check if this script is ready to execute and what values the options/flags are
  ClaudeHook check
* use ClaudeHook env to generate an example .env file
  ClaudeHook env > .env
* use ClaudeHook update to update to the latest version
  ClaudeHook update
* >>> bash script created with pforret/bashew
* >>> for bash development, also check out pforret/setver and pforret/progressbar

items.title: ⚡️ Examples
items.text:
> ClaudeHook -h 
# get extended usage info
> ClaudeHook env > .env
# create a .env file with default values

items.title: 🚀 Installation
items.text:
with basher

$ basher install pforret/ClaudeHook

or with git

$ git clone https://github.com/pforret/ClaudeHook.git
$ cd ClaudeHook

items.title: 📝 Acknowledgements
items.text:
• script created with bashew

© 2026 Peter Forret

items.title: Markdown Extensions
items.text:
Note

Admonitions are a great way to make notes to your readers. This is a note.

Button

import tensorflow as tf # code block
Block1Block2

Text 1

Text 2

Text can be {--deleted--} and replacement text {++added++}. This can also be combined into {one~>a single} operation. {Highlighting} is also possible {>>and comments can be added inline<<}.

  • Grid 1
  • Grid 2
  • Grid 3
  • Grid 4

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed neque elit,

  • Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit
  • Vestibulum convallis sit amet nisi a tincidunt
    • In hac habitasse platea dictumst
    • In scelerisque nibh non dolor mollis congue sed et metus
    • Praesent sed risus massa
  • Aenean pretium efficitur erat, donec pharetra, ligula non scelerisque

items.title: Installed plugins
items.text:
• search (built-in)
  • blog (built-in)
  • awesome-pages

  • with-pdf

    for demo purposes - you can easily delete this file

items.title: 1. options/flags/usage
items.text:
bashew only needs one specification of flags, options and parameters, and will use this to do the parsing as well as showing the usage.

This is specified in the beginning of the script as 

#commented lines will be filtered
flag|h|help|show usage
flag|q|quiet|no output
flag|v|verbose|output more
flag|f|force|do not ask for confirmation (always yes)
option|l|log_dir|folder for log files |$HOME/log/$script_prefix
#option|w|width|width to use|800
#list|u|user|user(s) to execute this for
param|1|action|action to perform: analyze/convert
param|?|input|input file

items.title: flag
items.text:
-v|--verbose : [flag] output more [default: off]

  • a 'flag' is a boolean variable. Default is false/off.
  • a flag always has a short (-v) and a long (--verbose) name
  • the flag's value will be available as the $verbose variable in the script.
  • flags can also be set in a .env file (see 3.)

items.title: option
items.text:
-l|--log_dir <?> : [option] folder for log files [default: $HOME/log/normal]

  • an 'option' is a text variable. Default is specified in the option definition (last field).
  • an option always has a short (-l) and a long (--logdir) name
  • the option's value will be available as the $logdir variable in the script.
  • options can also be set in a .env file (see 3.)

items.title: param
items.text:
<action>         : [parameter] action to perform: analyze/convert
<input>          : [parameter] input file (optional)
* a 'param' is a text variable. They can be required or optional * a param has only a long (action) name * the param's value will be available as the $action variable in the script. * params cannot be specified in the .env file

items.title: 2. .env support
items.text:
bashew will auto-detect and import .env files. A .env file is just a series of variable definitions (variable="value"). They contain presets that are tuned to this user or this machine, and they can contain secrets (passwords, API keys ...) .env files are never added to a git repository.

A bashew-based script myscript.sh will look for the following .env files (in this order) * [script_installation_path]/.env * [script_installation_path]/myscript.env * [script_installation_path]/.myscript.env * ./.env * ./myscript.env * ./.myscript.env

This allows for * 'global settings' in [script_installation_path] and 'local settings' in the current folder * separate settings for aliases (symbolic links) of the script. E.g. calling a script as deploy_dev could use other default settings than deploy_prod

items.title: system detection, script resolving
items.text:
bashew can detect the system you're running on, and the actual folder the script is installed in. This is important if the script needs to find other files (templates, imported files) in the same folder. This is done by recursively resolving symbolic links until the actual real script file is found.

# 🔎 Script path: $HOME/.basher/cellar/bin/note 
# 🔎 Symbolic ln: $HOME/.basher/cellar/bin/note -> [$HOME/.basher/cellar/packages/pforret/note/note] 
# 🔎 Symbolic ln: $HOME/.basher/cellar/packages/pforret/note/note -> [./note.sh] 
# 🔎 Actual path: $HOME/.basher/cellar/packages/pforret/note/note.sh 
# 🔎 Shell type : bash - version 5.1.4(1)-release 
# 🔎 System OS  : macOS (Darwin) 11.2 on x86_64 
# 🔎 Package mgt: brew install 
# 🔎 Last modif : Feb 11 10:04:40 2021 
# 🔎 Script ID  : 811 lines / md5: df13c117 
# 🔎 Creation   : 2020-12-14 
# 🔎 Running as : pforret@MacBook-Pro.forret

items.title: 4. dependencies
items.text:
bashew scripts can specify the programs/binaries/packages they depend upon and explain the user how to install them if they cannot be found.

This is done in the beginning of the script: 

ffmpeg
convert|imagemagick
progressbar|basher install pforret/progressbar
* if ffmpeg cannot be found: tell user to install it by using brew/apt/yum/... install ffmpeg (depending on the OS) * if convert cannot be found: tell user to do install by using brew/apt/yum/... install imagemagick (depending on the OS) * if progressbar cannot be found: tell user to do install by using basher install pforret/progressbar

items.title: bash_unit
items.text:
• unit testing is done with bash_unit
  • all test scripts are in the tests folder
  • tests can be run by calling tests/run_tests.sh
  • tests are also run upon commit/push (see .github/bash_unit.yml)

items.title: Shellcheck
items.text:
• source code linting/checking is done with shellcheck
  • shellcheck can be run by calling shellcheck *.sh
  • shellcheck is also run upon commit/push (see .github/shellcheck.yml)

items.title: Getting started
items.text:
• edit the source Markdown files in the /docs folder
  • run mkdox serve to run it locally with real-time updates when you change anything
  • run mkdox build to generate the whole site in the /site folder, ready to publish

items.title: Features
items.text:
• special mkdocs/material features:
    • navigation with search
    • responsive design
    • code blocks with syntax highlighting
    • callouts, admonitions
    • tables, images, links
    • built-in SEO features
    • check extensions and plugins for more details

created on 2026-05-21 by pforret

items.title: 2026 05 21.claudehook say and install
items.text: name: "ClaudeHook say + install — voice notifications wired into Claude Code hooks" description: |

items.title: Purpose
items.text:
Add two new verbs to ClaudeHook.sh so the tool becomes useful end-to-end:

  1. ClaudeHook say <whatever> — speaks "<APP_NAME> <whatever>" out loud (macOS say / Linux spd-say/espeak, echo fallback). APP_NAME is taken from .env if present, otherwise from basename $PWD.
  2. ClaudeHook install — interactive installer that (a) asks global (~/.claude/settings.json) vs project only (./.claude/settings.json), then (b) prompts the user to install five Claude Code hooks with sensible defaults that each call back into ClaudeHook say …: Notification, Stop, StopFailure, PreCompact, PermissionRequest.

The result: after one ClaudeHook install, the user hears their machine speak the project name whenever Claude needs attention, finishes, fails, compacts, or asks for permission.

items.title: Core Principles
items.text:
1. Match bashew conventions — add verbs to Option:config() choice line, add case blocks in Script:main(), implement do_<verb>() helpers, use IO:* for output and Os:require for binaries.
  2. No new abstractions — this is a single-file bash script. No new files except optional tests. Keep functions short, no premature library.
  3. Idempotent installs — re-running install must not duplicate hook entries in settings.json. Use jq for safe JSON merges.
  4. Testable in CI without speaking — fall back to echo when no TTS binary is present (Linux CI has none), and provide a --DRY_RUN flag so a developer on macOS can verify the spoken text without audio.

items.title: Goal
items.text:
After this PRP is implemented, the following user journeys work end-to-end:

# 1) Speak a custom message (used by the hooks, but also directly callable)
$ cd ~/Code/myproject
$ ClaudeHook say "is done"
# → speaks: "myproject is done"   (basename $PWD when no .env)

$ echo 'APP_NAME=Hermes' > .env
$ ClaudeHook say "needs your attention"
# → speaks: "Hermes needs your attention"

# 2) Install hooks interactively
$ ClaudeHook install
Install hooks for: [1] this project only  [2] global (all projects) > 2
Install Notification hook?       (will say "<app> needs your attention") [y/N] y
Install Stop hook?               (will say "<app> is done") [y/N] y
Install StopFailure hook?        (will say "<app> encountered an error") [y/N] y
Install PreCompact hook?         (will say "<app> is compacting context") [y/N] n
Install PermissionRequest hook?  (will say "<app> needs permission") [y/N] y
✅ 4 hooks installed in /Users/pforret/.claude/settings.json

# 3) Force mode bypasses prompts and installs all hooks globally
$ ClaudeHook -f install
✅ 5 hooks installed in /Users/pforret/.claude/settings.json

items.title: Why
items.text:
• The bashew template in ClaudeHook.sh currently has only placeholder verbs (action1, action2). The repo name promises Claude-Code-hook functionality but the script does nothing useful yet.
  • Voice notifications for hook events is a high-value, low-complexity Claude Code use case that this tool can own.
  • One install command is dramatically easier than hand-editing JSON in ~/.claude/settings.json.

items.title: User-visible behavior
items.text:
ClaudeHook say [message] - Resolves the app name in this order: $APP_NAME (loaded automatically by bashew's Os:import_env from .env) → basename "$PWD". - Speaks "<app_name> <message>" using the first available of: say (macOS), spd-say, espeak (Linux). If none are available or --DRY_RUN is set, prints the message to stdout instead. - Empty message is allowed (just speaks the app name). Useful for testing. - Multi-word messages must be quoted on the CLI (consistent with bashew's param|? single optional positional).

ClaudeHook install - Asks: project (./.claude/settings.json) vs global (~/.claude/settings.json). Uses IO:question with a default of project. - For each of the 5 hooks, prompts y/N via IO:confirm. With -f/--FORCE all prompts auto-yes (this is the documented bashew IO:confirm behavior). - The command written into settings.json is the absolute path to ClaudeHook.sh (resolved via $script_install_path, already computed by Script:meta), so the hook works regardless of $PATH. - Hook entries are merged into existing settings.json via jq. Re-running does not create duplicates (the implementation removes any existing entry with an identical command string before appending).

items.title: Success Criteria
items.text:
• ClaudeHook say "hello" in a directory with no .env says " hello" (or prints it on Linux without TTS).
  • ClaudeHook say "hello" in a directory whose .env contains APP_NAME=Foo says "Foo hello".
  • ClaudeHook install (interactive) creates a valid ~/.claude/settings.json or ./.claude/settings.json containing the chosen hooks under .hooks.<EventName>[].
  • Each installed hook entry has shape {"type": "command", "command": "<abs-path>/ClaudeHook.sh say \"<message>\"", "timeout": 10}.
  • Running ClaudeHook -f install twice in a row results in the same final JSON (idempotent — no duplicate entries).
  • The resulting settings.json is valid JSON (jq . settings.json succeeds).
  • shellcheck ClaudeHook.sh produces no new warnings.
  • bash_unit tests/test_* passes, including new tests for say and install.
  • Help output (ClaudeHook --help) lists say and install as actions, and the #TIP: lines render in the tips section.

items.title: Documentation & References
items.text:
- url: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md
  why: Canonical list of hook event names and JSON I/O contract.
  critical: |
    The five events we install ARE real and spelled exactly:
      Notification, Stop, StopFailure, PreCompact, PermissionRequest.
    Hooks receive JSON on stdin (we don't need it for plain `say`).
    Exit 0 = success. Exit 2 = blocking error (we never want this).

- url: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md
  why: settings.json schema and scope precedence.
  critical: |
    Global file: ~/.claude/settings.json
    Project file: <project>/.claude/settings.json (note the .claude/ prefix)
    Schema:
      { "hooks": { "<EventName>": [ { "type": "command", "command": "<shell>", "timeout": <int seconds> } ] } }

- file: /Users/pforret/Code/pforret/ClaudeHook/ClaudeHook.sh
  why: |
    The whole framework lives here. Specifically:
      - Option:config()   lines 64–91  → add verbs to the `choice|1|action|...` line
      - Script:main()     lines 97–148 → add case blocks
      - do_action1/2()    lines 157–170 → mirror this signature for do_say / do_install
      - IO:confirm        line 317     → y/N prompt that returns 0 on yes; -f skips to yes
      - IO:question       line 324     → free-form prompt with default
      - $script_install_path           → absolute path to ClaudeHook.sh, set in Script:meta
      - Os:require "jq"                → guards against missing jq, with FORCE auto-install

- file: /Users/pforret/Code/pforret/ClaudeHook/tests/test_basic.sh
  why: bash_unit pattern; mirror this for new test files.

- doc: https://github.com/pgrange/bash_unit
  section: README → assertions
  critical: Tests must start with `test_`. Use `assert`, `assert_equals`, `assert_fails`.

- doc: https://stedolan.github.io/jq/manual/
  section: Update operators (`|=`, `+=`)
  critical: |
    To append without duplicating:
      jq --arg cmd "$cmd" '.hooks.Stop |= (map(select(.command != $cmd)) + [{type:"command", command:$cmd, timeout:10}])'
    `.hooks.Stop // []` provides an empty array if the key is missing.

items.title: Current Codebase tree
items.text:
ClaudeHook/
├── ClaudeHook.sh                  # bashew-generated single-file script (1137 lines)
├── ClaudeHook -> ./ClaudeHook.sh  # symlink
├── CLAUDE.md
├── README.md
├── VERSION.md                     # 0.0.1
├── docs/
│   ├── bashew.md, features.md, functions.md, index.md
│   └── todo/PRPs/                 # ← this PRP lives here
├── tests/
│   ├── run_tests.sh
│   └── test_basic.sh              # mirror this for new tests
└── .github/workflows/             # bash_unit.yml + shellcheck.yml run on every push

items.title: Desired Codebase tree (after this PRP)
items.text:
ClaudeHook/
├── ClaudeHook.sh                  # MODIFIED: 2 new verbs, 2 new do_* functions, tips updated
├── tests/
│   ├── test_basic.sh              # unchanged
│   ├── test_say.sh                # NEW: tests for `say` verb (uses --DRY_RUN)
│   └── test_install.sh            # NEW: tests for `install` verb (uses TMPDIR for settings.json)
└── (no other new files — keep it lean, no separate lib/)

items.title: Known Gotchas of our codebase & Library Quirks
items.text:
# CRITICAL: bashew strict mode is `set -uo pipefail` (no -e). Trap on EXIT runs IO:die on any
# non-zero exit from a *piped* command. Read ClaudeHook.sh line 195 and 496–499.
# → Inside do_install(), prefer `if ! cmd; then …; fi` over relying on $? after a pipeline.

# CRITICAL: Os:import_env (line 1062) runs BEFORE Option:parse and BEFORE Script:main.
# So $APP_NAME from .env is already in the environment when do_say() runs. Don't re-source.

# CRITICAL: bashew uses `param|?|input|input file/text` (line 90). $input is a single optional
# positional. Multi-word messages MUST be quoted by the caller:
#   ClaudeHook say "needs attention"     ← correct
#   ClaudeHook say needs attention       ← bashew dies with "cannot interpret extra parameters"
# This is intentional — hooks always pass a quoted message, so it's the natural shape.

# CRITICAL: $script_install_path (set in Script:meta, line 952–957) is the symlink-resolved
# absolute path to ClaudeHook.sh. Use this for the hook `command` field — NOT $0, which could
# be a relative path or the symlink "./ClaudeHook".

# CRITICAL: IO:confirm (line 317) returns 0 on Y, 1 on N. With -f/--FORCE it auto-returns 0.
# This means `ClaudeHook -f install` should install ALL hooks without prompting. Good for CI.

# CRITICAL: jq is not always installed. Os:require "jq" (with no second arg) will suggest
# `brew install jq` on macOS and `apt install jq` on Debian/Ubuntu via $install_package.
# Don't try to hand-roll JSON editing with sed — settings.json may have unrelated keys we
# must preserve verbatim.

# GOTCHA: The macOS `say` command speaks asynchronously and returns immediately. The Linux
# `spd-say` does too. `espeak` is synchronous. For hooks we want non-blocking so Claude
# doesn't wait — backgrounding (`&`) is safer cross-platform but interferes with our test
# harness. Compromise: run the TTS binary in the foreground; users on espeak can add `&` in
# their settings.json command if they want.

# GOTCHA: On the bashew CI runner (Linux, no TTS), `command -v say` and `command -v spd-say`
# both return empty. The fallback `echo` path is what tests exercise. Keep that branch clean.

# GOTCHA: When writing to ~/.claude/settings.json, create the parent dir if it doesn't exist
# (`mkdir -p`). For project scope, write to ./.claude/settings.json (note: the directory is
# .claude, not claude — and it must be created if absent).

items.title: Data shapes
items.text:
The five hook events and their default spoken messages (used by do_install):

# Constant table — declare in do_install() as parallel arrays (bash 3-safe; macOS default bash is 3.2).
# A bash 4 associative array would be cleaner but breaks on stock macOS.

hook_events=("Notification" "Stop" "StopFailure" "PreCompact" "PermissionRequest")
hook_messages=(
  "needs your attention"
  "is done"
  "encountered an error"
  "is compacting context"
  "needs permission"
)
hook_timeout=10

Resulting JSON for one hook entry: 

{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "/Users/pforret/Code/pforret/ClaudeHook/ClaudeHook.sh say \"is done\"",
  "timeout": 10
}

items.title: List of tasks to be completed (in order)
items.text:
Task 1 — Wire the two new verbs into Option:config():
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - FIND line 88: 'choice|1|action|action to perform|action1,action2,check,env,update'
  - REPLACE with: 'choice|1|action|action to perform|say,install,check,env,update'
  - REASON: action1/action2 are vestigial bashew placeholders. Replace them outright rather
    than appending — this is the project's own script, not a generic template, and the help
    output should advertise what the tool actually does.

Task 2 — Add case branches in Script:main():
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - FIND the `case "${action,,}" in` block (line 105).
  - REMOVE the action1/action2/action3 case blocks (lines 106–123).
  - ADD two new case blocks BEFORE the `check | env)` block:
      say)
        #TIP: use «$script_prefix say <message>» to speak "<app> <message>"
        #TIP:> $script_prefix say "is done"
        do_say
        ;;
      install)
        #TIP: use «$script_prefix install» to install Claude Code hooks (interactive)
        #TIP:> $script_prefix install
        do_install
        ;;
  - PRESERVE the `check | env`, `update`, and `*` (die) blocks verbatim.

Task 3 — Implement do_say():
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - REMOVE the placeholder do_action1() and do_action2() functions (lines 157–170).
  - ADD do_say() following bashew helper conventions (IO:log first, IO:die on hard errors).
  - LOGIC:
      1. Resolve app_name: prefer ${APP_NAME:-}, else basename "$PWD".
      2. Build full_message: "$app_name $input"  (or just "$app_name" if $input is empty).
      3. If DRY_RUN is set OR no TTS binary found, IO:print "$full_message" and return.
      4. Else, pick the first available TTS: say > spd-say > espeak > IO:print fallback.
      5. Run it with the message as a single quoted arg.
  - PSEUDOCODE: see "Per task pseudocode" below.

Task 4 — Add the --DRY_RUN flag in Option:config():
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - FIND in Option:config() the flag block (lines 82–87).
  - ADD a new flag line BEFORE the LOG_DIR option line:
      flag|D|DRY_RUN|print message instead of speaking (for testing)
  - VERIFY shellcheck still passes (Option:initialize will auto-create $DRY_RUN=0).

Task 5 — Implement do_install():
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - ADD do_install() after do_say().
  - LOGIC:
      1. Os:require "jq"  — die early if jq missing (FORCE will auto-install).
      2. Ask scope via IO:question (default: project). Accept "g" / "global" / "p" / "project"
         OR a numeric 1/2.
      3. Compute target settings.json path:
           global  → "$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
           project → "./.claude/settings.json"
         mkdir -p the parent dir.
      4. If file doesn't exist, write '{"hooks":{}}' as the seed.
      5. For each of the 5 events, call IO:confirm with a descriptive prompt. If yes:
           - Build cmd = "$script_install_path say \"$default_message\""
           - jq-merge into .hooks.<event>, dedup by .command.
      6. Validate final JSON with `jq . "$target"` and IO:success on completion.

Task 6 — Add tests/test_say.sh:
CREATE tests/test_say.sh:
  - MIRROR pattern from tests/test_basic.sh (root_folder / root_script discovery).
  - Test 1: `say "hello"` with empty .env prints "$(basename $PWD) hello" (use --DRY_RUN).
  - Test 2: `say "hello"` with APP_NAME=Foo in env prints "Foo hello".
  - Test 3: `say` with empty input prints just the app name.
  - Use `assert_equals` on captured output.
  - IMPORTANT: tests must run from tests/ directory; use a tmpdir so .env doesn't pollute.

Task 7 — Add tests/test_install.sh:
CREATE tests/test_install.sh:
  - Test 1: `ClaudeHook -f install` (FORCE → all hooks yes) writing to a custom path.
    → How: set HOME=$(mktemp -d) so ~/.claude/settings.json lands in tmpdir.
    → Pipe "2\n" as scope answer (global). Or pre-fill the scope by env var (see Task 8).
    → Assert: jq '.hooks | keys | length' == 5 on the resulting file.
  - Test 2: Re-run install with same options → assert no duplicates (each hook array len == 1).
  - Test 3: Resulting JSON is valid (`jq . settings.json` exits 0).

Task 8 — (Optional but recommended) Non-interactive scope flag:
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh do_install:
  - ALLOW the scope to be set via $input when action == install:
      ClaudeHook install global   → no prompt, global scope
      ClaudeHook install project  → no prompt, project scope
      ClaudeHook install          → interactive prompt
  - REASON: makes Task 7 tests trivial and gives power users a one-liner.
  - The existing `param|?|input` already accepts this — just interpret $input inside do_install.

Task 9 — Update help/tips and README:
MODIFY ClaudeHook.sh:
  - Verify the #TIP comments added in Task 2 render correctly by running `ClaudeHook --help`.
MODIFY README.md:
  - Replace the auto-generated action1/action2 examples in the usage block with `say` and `install`.
  - Add a short "Setup" section showing `ClaudeHook install` as the one-step onboarding.
  - KEEP the badges, installation, acknowledgements sections.

Task 10 — Run validation gates and fix iteratively (see Validation Loop below).

items.title: Per task pseudocode
items.text:
Task 3 — do_say body: 

function do_say() {
  IO:log "say: '$input'"

  local app_name
  if [[ -n "${APP_NAME:-}" ]]; then
    app_name="$APP_NAME"
  else
    app_name="$(basename "$PWD")"
  fi

  local full_message
  if [[ -n "${input:-}" ]]; then
    full_message="$app_name $input"
  else
    full_message="$app_name"
  fi

  # DRY_RUN or no TTS available → just print.
  if ((DRY_RUN)); then
    IO:print "$full_message"
    return 0
  fi

  if command -v say >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    say "$full_message"
  elif command -v spd-say >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    spd-say "$full_message"
  elif command -v espeak >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    espeak "$full_message" 2>/dev/null
  else
    # No TTS — degrade to stdout so the hook still produces visible feedback.
    IO:print "$full_message"
  fi
}

Task 5 — do_install body: 

function do_install() {
  IO:log "install"
  Os:require "jq"

  # 1) Resolve scope (allow CLI override via $input).
  local scope target_file target_dir
  case "${input:-}" in
    g|global|G|GLOBAL) scope="global" ;;
    p|project|P|PROJECT) scope="project" ;;
    "")
      local answer
      answer="$(IO:question "Install hooks for: [p]roject only / [g]lobal" "p")"
      case "${answer,,}" in
        g|global|2) scope="global" ;;
        *)          scope="project" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    *) IO:die "unknown scope [$input] — use 'global' or 'project'" ;;
  esac

  if [[ "$scope" == "global" ]]; then
    target_dir="$HOME/.claude"
  else
    target_dir="./.claude"
  fi
  target_file="$target_dir/settings.json"
  mkdir -p "$target_dir"

  # 2) Seed empty settings file if absent.
  if [[ ! -f "$target_file" ]]; then
    echo '{"hooks":{}}' > "$target_file"
    IO:debug "Created empty $target_file"
  fi

  # 3) Loop over hook events.
  local hook_events=("Notification" "Stop" "StopFailure" "PreCompact" "PermissionRequest")
  local hook_messages=("needs your attention" "is done" "encountered an error" "is compacting context" "needs permission")
  local installed=0
  local i event message cmd

  for i in "${!hook_events[@]}"; do
    event="${hook_events[$i]}"
    message="${hook_messages[$i]}"
    cmd="$script_install_path say \"$message\""

    if IO:confirm "Install $event hook? (will say \"<app> $message\")"; then
      # Merge into .hooks.$event, deduping by .command.
      local tmpfile
      tmpfile="$(Os:tempfile json)"
      jq \
        --arg event "$event" \
        --arg cmd "$cmd" \
        --argjson timeout 10 \
        '
          .hooks //= {} |
          .hooks[$event] = ((.hooks[$event] // []) | map(select(.command != $cmd)))
                          + [{type:"command", command:$cmd, timeout:$timeout}]
        ' \
        "$target_file" > "$tmpfile" || IO:die "jq failed updating $target_file"
      mv "$tmpfile" "$target_file"
      installed=$((installed + 1))
      IO:debug "Installed $event → $cmd"
    fi
  done

  # 4) Final validate.
  if jq -e . "$target_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    IO:success "$installed hook(s) installed in $target_file"
  else
    IO:die "settings.json is not valid JSON after edits: $target_file"
  fi
}

items.title: Integration Points
items.text:
SETTINGS.JSON (Claude Code):
  - global: $HOME/.claude/settings.json
  - project: ./.claude/settings.json
  - schema: {"hooks": {"<EventName>": [{"type":"command","command":"…","timeout":10}]}}
  - dedup key: the `.command` string (so re-runs are idempotent)

ENV:
  - APP_NAME — read from .env (auto-loaded by bashew Os:import_env). Falls back to basename $PWD.
  - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — Claude Code sets this; basename $PWD inside the hook context is
    equivalent and simpler. We do NOT need to read this explicitly.

OPTION:CONFIG (bashew):
  - choice|1|action|action to perform|say,install,check,env,update
  - flag|D|DRY_RUN|print message instead of speaking (for testing)
  - param|?|input|input file/text  ← already exists; reused for `say <msg>` and `install <scope>`

items.title: Level 1: Syntax & Style
items.text:
# Static analysis — bashew CI runs this on every push (.github/workflows/shellcheck.yml).
shellcheck ClaudeHook.sh
# Expected: same warning level as before this PRP. New code must not add new SC* warnings.

# Bash syntax-only check (no execution):
bash -n ClaudeHook.sh
# Expected: empty output, exit 0.

items.title: Level 2: Unit tests (bash_unit)
items.text:
# Install bash_unit if missing:
#   macOS: brew install bash_unit
#   Other: https://github.com/pgrange/bash_unit

# Run the existing + new test files:
tests/run_tests.sh
# OR directly:
bash_unit -f tap tests/test_*

# Expected output: all tests pass (TAP "ok" lines), no "not ok".

Tests to write (in tests/test_say.sh and tests/test_install.sh, mirroring tests/test_basic.sh):

# tests/test_say.sh
root_folder=$(cd .. && pwd)
root_script=$(find "$root_folder" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.sh" | head -1)

test_say_uses_basename_when_no_env() {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  ( cd "$tmpdir" && assert_equals \
      "$(basename "$tmpdir") hello" \
      "$("$root_script" -D say hello)" )
}

test_say_uses_app_name_from_env() {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  echo 'APP_NAME=Hermes' > "$tmpdir/.env"
  ( cd "$tmpdir" && assert_equals \
      "Hermes hello" \
      "$("$root_script" -D say hello)" )
}

test_say_empty_message_prints_just_app_name() {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  echo 'APP_NAME=Foo' > "$tmpdir/.env"
  ( cd "$tmpdir" && assert_equals "Foo" "$("$root_script" -D say)" )
}

# tests/test_install.sh
root_folder=$(cd .. && pwd)
root_script=$(find "$root_folder" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.sh" | head -1)

test_install_force_global_creates_all_hooks() {
  local fake_home
  fake_home="$(mktemp -d)"
  HOME="$fake_home" "$root_script" -f install global >/dev/null
  local settings="$fake_home/.claude/settings.json"
  assert "test -f $settings"
  assert_equals 5 "$(jq '.hooks | keys | length' "$settings")"
}

test_install_is_idempotent() {
  local fake_home
  fake_home="$(mktemp -d)"
  HOME="$fake_home" "$root_script" -f install global >/dev/null
  HOME="$fake_home" "$root_script" -f install global >/dev/null
  local settings="$fake_home/.claude/settings.json"
  # Each event's array should have exactly 1 entry, not 2.
  assert_equals 1 "$(jq '.hooks.Stop | length' "$settings")"
  assert_equals 1 "$(jq '.hooks.Notification | length' "$settings")"
}

test_install_produces_valid_json() {
  local fake_home
  fake_home="$(mktemp -d)"
  HOME="$fake_home" "$root_script" -f install global >/dev/null
  assert "jq -e . $fake_home/.claude/settings.json >/dev/null"
}

items.title: Level 3: Manual smoke test
items.text:
# 1) Basic say
cd /tmp && mkdir -p smoke && cd smoke
/path/to/ClaudeHook.sh -D say "is ready"
# Expected stdout: "smoke is ready"

echo 'APP_NAME=Demo' > .env
/path/to/ClaudeHook.sh -D say "is ready"
# Expected stdout: "Demo is ready"

# 2) Install (interactive — answer prompts manually)
/path/to/ClaudeHook.sh install
# Walk through prompts, then verify:
jq '.hooks' ./.claude/settings.json

# 3) Real hook firing — install globally, start Claude Code in any project,
#    run a trivial prompt to completion. The macOS `say` voice should pronounce
#    "<project> is done" when Claude finishes.

items.title: Final validation Checklist
items.text:
• shellcheck ClaudeHook.sh — no new warnings.
  • bash -n ClaudeHook.sh — exits 0.
  • tests/run_tests.sh — all tests pass, including new test_say.sh and test_install.sh.
  • ClaudeHook --help lists say and install in the action choices, and TIPS section shows both.
  • ClaudeHook -D say "is done" in a /tmp/foo dir prints "foo is done".
  • HOME=$(mktemp -d) ClaudeHook -f install global produces a valid settings.json with 5 hook arrays of length 1.
  • Running the install twice does not duplicate entries.
  • jq is added to required commands (verify with ClaudeHook check).
  • README.md usage block reflects the real actions, not the bashew placeholder actions.
  • CI passes (.github/workflows/bash_unit.yml + shellcheck.yml).

items.title: Anti-Patterns to Avoid
items.text:
• ❌ Don't introduce a separate lib/ folder or split into multiple files — bashew's whole value is a single self-contained script.
  • ❌ Don't hand-edit JSON with sed/awk — use jq and require it explicitly. The dedup logic is impossible to do safely without a JSON parser.
  • ❌ Don't background the TTS command (say "$msg" &) inside do_say — let users decide via their own settings.json if they want non-blocking.
  • ❌ Don't read $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to compute the app name — basename $PWD and .env already cover both interactive and hook-invoked contexts, and the cwd is what bashew's env loader keys off.
  • ❌ Don't keep action1/action2 "for backwards compatibility" — the script has zero users yet (initial commit was 1 day ago) and the help text is part of the deliverable.
  • ❌ Don't add a --global / --project flag in addition to the positional install global argument — pick one. The positional is simpler and reuses existing $input.
  • ❌ Don't write to ~/.config/claude/... or any other path — the Claude Code docs are explicit that the file is ~/.claude/settings.json.
  • ❌ Don't try to install hooks by appending to a file (>>) — that produces invalid JSON the moment there's a second hook.

items.title: PRP Confidence Score: 9/10
items.text: This PRP should one-pass with a competent bash-aware AI agent because: - Every change is to a single file (ClaudeHook.sh) plus two greenfield test files. - The bashew framework is fully documented in the file's own header (lines 9–54). - Hook event names, JSON schema, and stdin contract are verified against the official Anthropic docs (May 2026). - Validation gates (shellcheck, bash_unit, manual smoke) are all locally runnable and CI-gated. - The only minor risk (-1 point) is bash 3 vs 4 array handling on stock macOS — the pseudocode uses parallel indexed arrays specifically to avoid bash-4-only associative arrays.
